title: kbs console status prints human-readable summary of all current UI state
description:
Given the console server is running with some UI state

When I run kbs console status

Then I see a human-readable summary showing:
  - Whether an issue is currently focused (and which one)
  - The current view mode (initiatives / epics / issues)
  - The current search query (if any)

When the console server is not running

Then I see 'Console server is not running.' and the command exits 0

Acceptance criteria:
- Output is human-readable (not raw JSON)
- All three state fields are shown, even if they are empty/default
- No error exit code when server is offline -- just prints the 'not running' message
creator: ryan.porter

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Implemented kbs console status command. Calls GET /api/ui-state, prints focus/view/search fields human-readably, or 'Console server is not running.' if server is offline.
